      "title": "BBC News gears up for Mega May election coverage",
      "content": "## Accessibility links. ### JavaScript has been disabled. #### JavaScript is required for the following feature(s):. Laura Kuenssberg and Sophie Raworth, along with BBC political editor Chris Mason, will be joined by a host of well-known BBC broadcasters for the network news results coverage of the most significant set of elections since the 2024 general election. On Thursday night, Laura Kuenssberg will be joined in the BBC’s London studio by Reeta Chakrabarti, who’ll be crunching the numbers as the polls close and results come in. Labour has been in power since the start of devolution, and a new voting system adds to the unpredictability of the results. From Edinburgh, Kirsty Wark will front network coverage of the Scottish Parliament elections, where the SNP has been in power for nearly 20 years. ### Useful links. ### Theme toggler.       "url": "https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c62nq678nyzo",
      "title": "What you need to know about the 7 May elections in England ... - BBC",
      "content": "# What you need to know about the 7 May elections in England, Scotland and Wales. The biggest set of elections since the 2024 general election will see millions of people head to the polls on 7 May. Voters in Scotland and Wales will elect representatives to their national parliaments, while a number of local council and mayoral polls will take place in England. In Northern Ireland, local council and Assembly elections are scheduled for May 2027. ## Which elections are taking place in England on 7 May? But in February, the government said elections would also go ahead in these areas on May, following legal advice in the wake of a challenge from Reform UK. ## Which elections are taking place in Scotland on 7 May? You must be 18 to vote in the local elections in England, or 16 to vote in the Scottish Parliament and Senedd elections.",
